title: myriocephalus appendiculatus
intro: Myriocephalus appendiculatus, also known as the prickly lettuce, is a flowering plant in the dandelion family that grows in the wild and is native to Australia and New Zealand. The medicinal properties of Myriocephalus appendiculatus include its ability to act as a laxative, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic. The health benefits of this plant include relief from constipation, menstrual cramps, and skin irritations, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Myriocephalus appendiculatus can be consumed as a tea, capsule, or tincture, with herbal preparations typically involving the roots and leaves of the plant.
